= Cross-Reference Demo

This fixture exercises cross-reference scroll landing against the sticky preview
header. Two kinds of cross-reference are covered:

* an *intra-document* cross-reference to a non-heading block (a table) anchored
  with `[#id]` — see <<metrics-table>>;
* an *inter-document* cross-reference into a deep section of a sibling document
  — see xref:xref-targets.adoc#deep-target[the deep target section].

Both targets must land below the bottom edge of the sticky preview header, not
behind it. A non-heading block is the interesting case: the offset that clears
the header must not be scoped to heading elements only.

== Metrics

Some prose introducing the data table. The table is anchored with `[#id]`, so
its id lands on a non-heading block — exactly the element type the header
clearance offset must still cover.

[#metrics-table]
.Benchmark Metrics
[cols="1,1",options="header"]
|===
|Key |Value
|alpha |1
|bravo |2
|charlie |3
|delta |4
|echo |5
|===

== Trailing content

The sections below exist solely to guarantee ample scrollable height beneath the
table target, so that clicking the intra-document cross-reference scrolls the
table all the way to the top of the viewport. Without enough trailing content
the scroll container bottoms out and the target never reaches the region behind
the sticky header — which would make the regression test pass trivially.

=== Filler one

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. The preview header is
sticky and translucent. A scroll into a target near the document end can only
clear the header if there is enough content beneath that target.

This paragraph adds vertical height. So does the next one.

=== Filler two

Repeated prose to add height. The exact words do not matter; the vertical extent
does. Each section pushes the bottom of the document further down.

More repeated prose. More height. The scroll container needs roughly a viewport
of content below the table for the table to reach the top edge.

=== Filler three

Still more prose. Still more height. Benchmarks, traceability, coverage, gaps,
confidence — synthetic words to fill vertical space deterministically.

Another paragraph of the same. And another, to be safe.

=== Filler four

Yet more prose so the table target can be positioned at the very top of the
scroll container, which is the only position that exposes header overlap.

A final filler paragraph in this section.

=== Filler five

The last filler section. By now there is well over a viewport of content beneath
the table, so the intra-document cross-reference scroll lands the table at the
top — clear of the header once the scroll-padding inset is in place.

One more paragraph to round things out.

Another closing paragraph of deterministic filler text.
